properties.name: Elena Voss
properties.bio: Founded Acme Co in 2019. Former VP Product at Datadog.

properties.name: James Wright
properties.bio: Leads all engineering teams. 28 indirect reports. Previously engineering director at Twilio.

properties.name: Lisa Huang
properties.bio: Leads the Platform team. K8s expert. Wrote the internal migration guide. 6 projects using K8s in last year.

properties.name: David Park
properties.bio: Manages Product Engineering team. 12 reports. Sarah Chen's manager.

properties.name: Maria Santos
properties.bio: Leads Customer Support organization. Jamie Chen's future manager.

properties.name: Mike Torres
properties.bio: 2.4 years tenure. Shipped K8s consolidation. Runs 3 production clusters. Mentors Derek and 2 other junior engineers. Working on payment migration.

properties.name: Ana Kim
properties.bio: 1.8 years tenure. Design system rewrite lead. Emerging bridge between engineering and design. 3 new cross-team edges with Design in 2 weeks.

properties.name: Raj Patel
properties.bio: 3.1 years tenure. Spreading thin — 4 active project edges up from 2. Collaboration shifted from deep to wide. 67 days without recognition. API refactor, observability dashboard, CI/CD, deployment tooling.

properties.name: Derek Lin
properties.bio: 11 months tenure. Accelerating — Go cert completed, paired with Mike twice, first solo PR. Graph density doubled since month 3.

properties.name: Sarah Okafor
properties.bio: 4 months tenure. Working solo on search service. 4 team edges + 1 cross-team — fewer than typical at 4 months (6-8 team + 2-3 cross-team).

properties.name: Chris Lee
properties.bio: Platform team member. Works on infrastructure automation.

properties.name: Priya Sharma
properties.bio: HR Manager under Operations. Currently on PTO through Feb 10. Also listed as recruiter for Jamie Chen in Greenhouse.

properties.name: Jamie Chen
properties.bio: Starting Mar 3. Onboarding 62% complete. Needs buddy assignment. Source: Greenhouse. Recruiter: Priya Sharma.

properties.name: Alex Rivera
properties.bio: Starting Feb 10. Benefits enrollment lagging — day 11, hasn't started enrollment. Reminder sent.

properties.name: Sam Okafor
properties.bio: Starting Feb 24 — referred to as Day 1 tomorrow in briefing. Zendesk and Jira access provisioned. 4 of 5 tools done. I-9 due day 2.

properties.name: Nina Kowalski
properties.bio: Started Nov 3, 2025. First 90 days. 90-day review upcoming (scheduled for ~Feb 6). Signed performance review acknowledgment.

properties.name: Sam Kim
properties.bio: 14 months tenure. AI-recommended buddy for Jamie Chen. Previous buddy scored 9/10 NPS.

properties.name: Taylor Brooks
properties.bio: Alternate buddy candidate for Jamie Chen. Support team member.

properties.name: Sarah Chen
properties.bio: 2.5 years tenure. Expecting a baby due Aug 15, 2026. PPO plan with Aetna. Manager: David Park. 14 PTO days available. FMLA eligible.

properties.bio: 18 months tenure. Elevated risk: workload spike + no recognition in 84 days.

properties.name: Derek Martinez
properties.bio: 11 months tenure. Elevated risk: PTO pattern change + close to exit tenure.

properties.name: Amy Lin
properties.bio: 8 months tenure. Workload above average. Elevated risk signal.

properties.name: Marcus Johnson
properties.bio: Accepted offer. Start Feb 24. Onboarding activated — 11 edges carried forward from ATS.

properties.name: James Liu
properties.bio: Interviewer. Rates 40% harder than average on design exercises. His last 5 'no hire' ratings — 3 were overturned and hired successfully.

properties.name: Aisha Obi
properties.notes: Strong systems design (9/10), solid references. 2-year employment gap (2022-2024), 'personal sabbatical.' Referrer Mike Torres vouches — they worked together at Stripe.

properties.name: Chris Wong
properties.notes: Conflicting feedback. Portfolio review: Ana Kim rated 'strong hire.' Design exercise: James Liu rated 'no hire' — weak interaction design.

properties.name: Maya Patel
properties.notes: Expected comp $185k. Role band $150-175k. 3 interviewers rated 'strong hire.' Market data supports $180k. First candidate all interviewers agree on. Phone screen tomorrow 2pm.

properties.name: Sarah Kim
properties.notes: Ready for offer. 4/4 interviewers positive. Passed technical screen. Onsite panel scheduled Tuesday. Reference check complete — 2/2 strong.

properties.name: Marcus Johnson
properties.notes: Accepted offer. Start Feb 24. Onboarding activated — 11 edges carried forward.

properties.name: Emily Tran
properties.notes: Final round Monday — panel with VP Eng + two leads.

properties.name: Tanya Brooks
properties.notes: Hasn't responded in 48 hours. Reminder sent.

properties.name: James Liu (candidate)
properties.notes: Onsite rescheduled from Thursday to Monday due to interviewer PTO conflict.

properties.feedback: Exceptional systems design. Deep knowledge of distributed systems. Designed a clean event-driven architecture for the whiteboard problem.
Good communication. Explained gap year thoughtfully. Strong collaboration examples from Stripe.
Outstanding portfolio. Clean, systematic approach to design systems. Strong visual hierarchy.
Weak interaction design. Missed several edge cases in the checkout flow exercise. Design aesthetics strong but functionality gaps.
Excellent coding skills. Solved the system design problem elegantly. Strong Go and infrastructure knowledge.
Deep understanding of distributed systems. Great approach to the caching problem.
Excellent leadership examples. Clear communication style. Would be a strong culture add.
4/4 positive. Strong across all technical dimensions. Excellent problem decomposition.
Solid technical fundamentals. Good approach to scaling challenges.
Great cultural fit. Strong examples of cross-functional collaboration.
2/2 references responded. Both rated candidate as top 10% of engineers they've worked with.
Good initial conversation. Strong sales background with enterprise experience.
Excellent closer. Strong relationship-building skills. Good territory management examples.

properties.recommendation: Scheduled: Final round panel with VP Eng + two leads, Monday 1pm

properties.feedback: Decent initial conversation but seemed distracted. Follow-up needed.

properties.name: Texas
properties.notes: No state income tax. Texas Workforce Commission for unemployment.

properties.name: Federal
properties.notes: Federal income tax, FICA, FUTA, ACA compliance.

properties.name: FMLA Leave
properties.summary: Up to 12 weeks job-protected leave for eligible employees (12+ months, 1,250+ hours). Concurrent with other leave types.

properties.name: Company Parental Leave
properties.summary: 4 weeks full pay parental leave, concurrent with FMLA. Available to all parents (birth, adoption, foster).

properties.name: Unpaid Extended Leave
properties.summary: Up to 16 weeks unpaid leave extension beyond FMLA. Manager and HR approval required.

properties.name: PTO Policy
properties.summary: Flexible PTO. Employees accrue 20 days/year for first 3 years, 25 days after. Can use for top-up during disability leave.

properties.name: Expense Policy — IC-4 Standard
properties.summary: IC-4 level: $2,500/month Brex corporate card limit. Standard expense categories apply.

properties.name: Harassment Prevention
properties.summary: Annual harassment prevention training required for all employees. Q1 deadline.

properties.name: Data Security
properties.summary: Annual data security training. Required for all employees with system access.

properties.name: Employee Handbook
properties.summary: Complete employee handbook. Updated annually. All new hires must acknowledge.

properties.name: Benefits Enrollment Window
properties.summary: New hires must complete benefits enrollment within 30 days of start date. Eligibility: immediate upon start.

properties.name: Remote Work Policy
properties.summary: Hybrid model: 3 days in office (Tu/We/Th), 2 remote (Mo/Fr). Full remote available with VP approval.

properties.name: Code of Conduct
properties.summary: Company code of conduct. All employees must acknowledge annually.

properties.details: Sarah Chen expecting a baby. Due Aug 15. FMLA eligible (2.5 years tenure). Planning leave Aug 15 — Nov 7. 4 weeks company parental at full pay, then STD + PTO top-up.

properties.summary: Nina Kowalski 90-day review. Draft ready from accumulated signals. Started Nov 3, 2025.
Nina Kowalski performance review acknowledgment signed Feb 5.
Mike Torres annual review. Shipped K8s consolidation, strong mentoring contributions.
Raj Patel annual review. High output across 4 projects. Reliability and technical depth recognized.
Ana Kim annual review. Strong design system work. Emerging cross-team influence.
Sarah Okafor 30-day check-in. Onboarding on track, starting search service project.

properties.message: Saved me two hours on the deployment pipeline issue. Knew exactly where to look.

Raj, I want to call out something I should have said sooner — you've been holding the line on the API refactor while picking up the observability dashboard and CI/CD work without missing a beat. That kind of reliability is what keeps this team moving. Thank you.
Mike has been incredibly patient walking me through the K8s cluster setup. I feel like I learned more in one pairing session than a week of docs.
Mike, shipping the K8s consolidation was a massive lift. Three production clusters, zero downtime migration. The whole platform team benefits from this work. Well done.
Ana's design system components saved the customer portal team at least a sprint of work. Everything just plugs in.
Maria, you handled the Saturday rush with a short crew and still hit every prep target. The weekend team runs because of you. Thank you. — Carlos
Derek's Go knowledge saved us from a nasty concurrency bug in the deployment tooling. Great catch.
Derek, your growth over the past few months has been remarkable. From pairing on everything to submitting your first solo PR — and a clean one at that. Keep it up.

properties.comment: Love the engineering culture. Great autonomy and interesting problems.
Best team I've worked on. Lisa is an amazing manager.
Solid company. Comp is fair, work is engaging.
Feeling stretched thin across too many projects. 4 active workstreams is not sustainable.
Design team is collaborative and creative. Kevin gives us real ownership.
Workload is unsustainable. We lost two people and haven't backfilled. I'm doing the work of three.
No career growth path. Been at the same level for 18 months with no conversation about what's next.
Support team is understaffed and underappreciated. Engineering gets all the recognition.
Great onboarding experience. Buddy program was really helpful.
Company is fine. Not bad, not great. Could use more transparency from leadership.
Love the technical challenges. Payment migration is hard but rewarding.
Good company, but I wish we had more face time with leadership. Feels siloed sometimes.
Feeling burned out. Haven't taken a real vacation in months and the queue never stops.
Sales team is great. Rachel is a strong leader and we have clear targets.
Stress level is high. Working late most nights. Worried about falling behind.
Struggling with work-life balance. The on-call rotation is brutal with the team this small.
Acme is where I want to build my career. Learning a ton.
Decent place. Wish the tooling was better — too much manual work in support.
Feeling isolated. Working solo on search service with little mentorship. Not sure I'm building the right thing.
This is the best job I've had. Clear mission, smart people, good benefits.
